subject: general-knowledge
description: Mountains & Plateaus MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Which plateau is widely known as the 'Roof of the World'?
mcqs.explanation: The Tibetan Plateau, with an average elevation of about 4,500 m, is the highest and largest plateau on Earth and is famously called the 'Roof of the World'.

mcqs.q: Which plateau, linking the Himalayas and Hindu Kush, is also called the 'Roof of the World'?
mcqs.explanation: The Pamir Plateau in Tajikistan/Afghanistan is the 'pamir knot' where the Himalayas, Karakoram, Hindu Kush, Kunlun and Tien Shan meet — also called the Roof of the World.

mcqs.q: The Deccan Plateau, known for its volcanic basalt layers, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: The Deccan Plateau covers most of peninsular India south of the Vindhyas, formed by massive volcanic eruptions ~66 million years ago that produced the Deccan Traps basalt.

mcqs.q: The Balochistan Plateau, rich in coal and chromite, is located in which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Balochistan Plateau covers most of Balochistan province in Pakistan. It is rich in coal, chromite, copper and natural gas — Pakistan's main mineral reserve.

mcqs.q: Which plateau in China is famous for its wind-blown fertile loam soil?
mcqs.explanation: The Loess Plateau in north-central China is famous for its yellow wind-blown silt (loess) — among the world's most fertile soils, but also highly erodible.

mcqs.q: The Anatolian Plateau is the central highland of which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Anatolian Plateau forms the central highland of Turkey, ringed by the Pontic Mountains in the north and the Taurus Mountains in the south.

mcqs.q: Which plateau includes the Dasht-e-Lut and Dasht-e-Kavir deserts?
mcqs.explanation: The Iranian Plateau spans Iran, Afghanistan and parts of Pakistan and contains Iran's two great deserts — Dasht-e-Kavir (salt) and Dasht-e-Lut (sand & lava).

mcqs.q: Which plateau is covered with steppe grasslands and has an extreme continental climate?
mcqs.explanation: The Mongolian Plateau spans Mongolia and Inner Mongolia (China). It is covered with steppe grasslands and experiences one of Earth's most extreme continental climates.

mcqs.q: The Yunnan Plateau, famous for its karst landscapes, is located in which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Yunnan-Guizhou Plateau in southwestern China is famous for its karst topography — including the Stone Forest and limestone pinnacles.

mcqs.q: Which plateau in Myanmar is noted for tea cultivation?
mcqs.explanation: The Shan Plateau in eastern Myanmar is known for its tea gardens, hill stations and its ethnically diverse population.

mcqs.q: The Columbia-Snake Plateau in the USA was formed by which process?
mcqs.explanation: The Columbia-Snake Plateau in the US Pacific Northwest was formed by massive flood-basalt eruptions about 17 million years ago — among the largest volcanic events in geological history.

mcqs.q: Which plateau is home to the world-famous Grand Canyon?
mcqs.explanation: The Colorado Plateau in the southwestern US (Arizona, Utah, Colorado, New Mexico) is home to the Grand Canyon and many other iconic national parks.

mcqs.q: Which plateau in Canada is also known as the 'Canadian Shield'?
mcqs.explanation: The Laurentian Plateau is the Canadian Shield — an ancient Precambrian craton covering most of eastern and central Canada, rich in minerals.

mcqs.q: The Ozark Plateau, known for its karst topography and forested hills, is located in which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Ozark Plateau spans Missouri, Arkansas and parts of Oklahoma and Kansas in the USA, famous for karst caves, springs and the Ozark Mountains.

mcqs.q: Which plateau contains the famous Chihuahua silver mines?
mcqs.explanation: The Mexican Plateau (Mesa Central) hosts the rich Chihuahua silver mines — Mexico is the world's largest silver producer.

mcqs.q: The Altiplano — the world's second-highest plateau — is located in:
mcqs.explanation: The Altiplano spans Bolivia, Peru, Chile and Argentina at ~3,750 m. It is the world's second-highest plateau after the Tibetan Plateau, and Lake Titicaca lies on it.

mcqs.q: The Brazilian Highlands cover most of which country's eastern part?
mcqs.explanation: The Brazilian Highlands occupy most of central and eastern Brazil — an ancient cratonic shield with savanna, hill country and major mineral wealth.

mcqs.q: Which plateau supports savanna used for cattle grazing across Brazil and Bolivia?
mcqs.explanation: The Mato Grosso (Matto Grosso) Plateau covers central-west Brazil and parts of Bolivia — vast savanna grasslands ideal for cattle ranching.

mcqs.q: Which plateau is home to Angel Falls, the world's highest waterfall?
mcqs.explanation: The Guiana (Guyana) Plateau in Venezuela is home to Angel Falls (979 m / 3,212 ft) — the world's highest uninterrupted waterfall, on Auyán-tepui.

mcqs.q: The Patagonian Plateau — a rain-shadow desert famous for sheep rearing — is located in:
mcqs.explanation: The Patagonian Plateau covers southern Argentina east of the Andes. It is a cold, dry rain-shadow desert and the world's largest sheep-rearing region.

mcqs.q: The Jos Plateau, known for tin mining, is located in which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Jos Plateau in central Nigeria is the centre of Nigerian tin mining and has a cool climate that made it a colonial-era hill station.

mcqs.q: The 'Roof of Africa' and source of the Blue Nile is which plateau?
mcqs.explanation: The Ethiopian Plateau (Highlands) — often called the 'Roof of Africa' — is the source of the Blue Nile, which contributes ~80% of the Nile's water during flood season.

mcqs.q: Which plateau in DR Congo is rich in copper and uranium?
mcqs.explanation: The Katanga Plateau in southern DR Congo is the heart of the Central African Copperbelt and was the source of uranium used in the first atomic bombs.

mcqs.q: The Ahaggar (Hoggar) Plateau is located in which desert?
mcqs.explanation: The Ahaggar (Hoggar) Plateau is a volcanic highland in southern Algeria within the Sahara Desert. Its highest peak is Mount Tahat (~2,908 m).

mcqs.q: The Meseta Central is the primary highland of which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Meseta Central forms most of the Iberian Peninsula in Spain — a high, dry tableland averaging 600–700 m, divided by the Central System mountains.

mcqs.q: Which Australian plateau contains diamond-bearing kimberlite rocks?
mcqs.explanation: The Kimberley Plateau in northwestern Australia contains the Argyle diamond mine — once the world's largest producer of diamonds, especially rare pink diamonds.

mcqs.q: Which is the world's highest and coldest ice-covered plateau?
mcqs.explanation: The Antarctic Plateau (~3,000+ m) at the heart of East Antarctica is the world's highest and coldest plateau, with surface temperatures recorded below -89°C.

mcqs.q: The oil and salt-rich Pothohar Plateau is located in which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Pothohar Plateau in northern Punjab (Pakistan) is famous for its oil fields and the Khewra Salt Mine — the second-largest salt mine in the world.

mcqs.q: Which mountain range is the highest and youngest fold-mountain system on Earth?
mcqs.explanation: The Himalayas — formed by the India-Eurasia collision ~50 million years ago — are the world's highest and youngest fold-mountain system, with 10 of the 14 'eight-thousanders'.

mcqs.q: K2, the world's second-highest peak, lies in which range?
mcqs.explanation: K2 (8,611 m) is located in the Karakoram Range on the Pakistan–China border. The Karakoram contains 4 of the world's 14 'eight-thousanders'.

mcqs.q: Tirich Mir is the highest peak of which mountain range?
mcqs.explanation: Tirich Mir (7,708 m) in Chitral, Pakistan, is the highest peak of the Hindu Kush range. The Hindu Kush stretches across Afghanistan and northern Pakistan.

mcqs.q: Which mountain range forms the northern boundary of the Tibetan Plateau?
mcqs.explanation: The Kunlun Mountains form the northern edge of the Tibetan Plateau and are one of the longest mountain chains in Asia (~3,000 km).

mcqs.q: Which range is known as the 'Celestial Mountains'?
mcqs.explanation: Tien Shan (literally 'Celestial Mountains' in Chinese) stretches across Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan and China — a major Central Asian range.

mcqs.q: Which range is an extension of the Himalayas into western Myanmar?
mcqs.explanation: The Arakan Yoma (Rakhine Range) in western Myanmar is the southern extension of the Himalayas, separating Myanmar from Bangladesh.

mcqs.q: Anai Mudi is the highest peak of which Indian mountain range?
mcqs.explanation: Anai Mudi (2,695 m) in Kerala is the highest peak of the Western Ghats and the highest point in peninsular India.

mcqs.q: The oil-rich Zagros Mountains are primarily located in which countries?
mcqs.explanation: The Zagros Mountains stretch ~1,600 km through Iran and northern Iraq. They sit atop some of the world's largest oil and gas reserves.

mcqs.q: Which range divides Europe and Asia and contains Mount Elbrus?
mcqs.explanation: The Caucasus Mountains divide Europe and Asia between the Black and Caspian seas. Mount Elbrus (5,642 m) in Russia is the highest peak in Europe.

mcqs.q: Mount Narodnaya is the highest peak of which boundary range?
mcqs.explanation: The Ural Mountains form the conventional boundary between Europe and Asia. Mount Narodnaya (1,895 m) is their highest peak.

mcqs.q: The iconic Mont Blanc belongs to which European mountain range?
mcqs.explanation: Mont Blanc (4,810 m) on the France–Italy border is the highest peak of the Alps and the highest in Western Europe.

mcqs.q: Which range forms the natural border between France and Spain?
mcqs.explanation: The Pyrenees stretch ~491 km across the France–Spain border, also bordering Andorra. Highest peak: Aneto (3,404 m).

mcqs.q: Which European range is described as 'crescent-shaped' and is rich in forests?
mcqs.explanation: The Carpathians form a 1,500 km crescent across Slovakia, Poland, Ukraine, Romania, Hungary and Serbia. Europe's largest virgin forests and brown-bear populations live here.

mcqs.q: Which range is often called the 'Spine of Italy'?
mcqs.explanation: The Apennines run nearly the full length of Italy — the 'Spine of Italy' — from Liguria to Calabria, with the Mount Etna and Vesuvius volcanic systems nearby.

mcqs.q: Galdhøpiggen is the highest peak of which range?
mcqs.explanation: Galdhøpiggen (2,469 m) in Norway is the highest peak of the Scandinavian Mountains — the range that forms the spine of the Scandinavian Peninsula.

mcqs.q: Which range is in Bulgaria and gave its name to the entire peninsula?
mcqs.explanation: The Balkan Mountains (Stara Planina) cross northern Bulgaria and give the Balkan Peninsula its name.

mcqs.q: What is the longest mountain range in Australia?
mcqs.explanation: The Great Dividing Range stretches ~3,500 km along Australia's east coast — the country's longest range and the 5th-longest land-based range in the world.

mcqs.q: Mount Cook is the highest peak of which range in New Zealand?
mcqs.explanation: Aoraki/Mount Cook (3,724 m) is New Zealand's highest peak and lies in the Southern Alps on the South Island.

mcqs.q: Which range holds the record as the longest continental mountain range on Earth?
mcqs.explanation: The Andes (~7,000 km) along South America's western coast are the longest continental mountain range in the world, passing through 7 countries.

mcqs.q: Mount Whitney is the highest peak of which California range?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Whitney (4,421 m) in California is the highest peak of the Sierra Nevada and the highest peak in the contiguous (lower-48) United States.

mcqs.q: The Rocky Mountains act as a continental divide in which region?
mcqs.explanation: The Rocky Mountains stretch ~4,800 km from Canada to New Mexico and form the Continental Divide of North America — separating Pacific-bound and Atlantic-bound rivers.

mcqs.q: Which ancient range in the Eastern USA is known for its coal deposits?
mcqs.explanation: The Appalachian Mountains — among the oldest ranges on Earth (~480 million years) — run along the eastern USA and Canada and hold vast coal reserves.

mcqs.q: What is the official height of Mount Everest?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Everest's official height was updated to 8,848.86 m (~8,849 m) by a joint Nepal-China survey in 2020. It is the world's highest peak.

mcqs.q: K2 is also known by which name?
mcqs.explanation: K2 is also called Mount Godwin-Austen (after the British surveyor Henry Godwin-Austen) and locally as Chogori — second-highest peak in the world.

mcqs.q: Which is the third-highest peak in the world?
mcqs.explanation: Kangchenjunga (8,586 m) on the Nepal-India border is the third-highest mountain in the world. Its name means 'Five Treasures of the Great Snow'.

mcqs.q: Which is the fifth-highest mountain in the world?
mcqs.explanation: Makalu (8,485 m) on the Nepal-Tibet border is the world's fifth-highest peak, known for its distinctive four-sided pyramid shape.

mcqs.q: Which 8,000 m peak is widely considered the easiest to climb?
mcqs.explanation: Cho Oyu (8,188 m) is widely considered the easiest of the 14 eight-thousanders due to its gentler slopes and accessibility — the 6th-highest peak.

mcqs.q: Dhaulagiri I means what in Sanskrit?
mcqs.explanation: Dhaulagiri (8,167 m) means 'White Mountain' in Sanskrit (dhavala = dazzling white, giri = mountain). It is the 7th-highest peak.

mcqs.q: Manaslu is the _____ highest mountain in the world.
mcqs.explanation: Manaslu (8,163 m) in Nepal's Mansiri Himal is the eighth-highest peak in the world. The name comes from Sanskrit 'Manasa' (intellect or soul).

mcqs.q: Which mountain is nicknamed the 'Killer Mountain'?
mcqs.explanation: Nanga Parbat (8,126 m) in Pakistan is called the 'Killer Mountain' due to its high climbing-death rate, especially during early 20th-century expeditions.

mcqs.q: Annapurna I was the first 8,000 m peak ever climbed — in which year?
mcqs.explanation: Annapurna I (8,091 m) was first climbed by Maurice Herzog and Louis Lachenal of France in June 1950 — making it the first 'eight-thousander' to be summited.

mcqs.q: Which is the highest peak in Europe?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Elbrus (5,642 m) in the Caucasus Mountains of Russia is the highest peak in Europe. Mont Blanc (4,810 m) is the highest in Western Europe.

mcqs.q: The iconic pyramid-shaped Matterhorn lies in which range?
mcqs.explanation: The Matterhorn (4,478 m) on the Switzerland-Italy border is one of the most photographed mountains in the world — a near-perfect pyramid in the Alps.

mcqs.q: Mount Kilimanjaro is located in which country?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Kilimanjaro (5,895 m) in Tanzania is the highest peak in Africa and the world's tallest free-standing mountain — a dormant stratovolcano.

mcqs.q: In which country is Mount Aconcagua located?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Aconcagua (6,961 m) in Argentina is the highest peak in South America and the highest mountain outside Asia.

mcqs.q: What is the world's highest active volcano?
mcqs.explanation: Ojos del Salado (6,893 m) on the Chile-Argentina border is the world's highest active volcano and the second-highest peak in the Western Hemisphere.

mcqs.q: Denali — North America's highest peak — was formerly known as:
mcqs.explanation: Denali (6,190 m) in Alaska was officially named Mount McKinley from 1917 until 2015, when it was renamed Denali (its indigenous Athabaskan name).

mcqs.q: Which is the highest peak in Mexico?
mcqs.explanation: Pico de Orizaba (5,636 m) is the highest peak in Mexico and the third-highest peak in North America — a dormant stratovolcano.

mcqs.q: Which peak is the highest in Antarctica?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Vinson (4,892 m) in the Ellsworth Mountains is the highest peak in Antarctica and one of the Seven Summits.

mcqs.q: Which mountain is the national symbol of Japan?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Fuji (3,776 m) is Japan's highest peak and an iconic national symbol — an active stratovolcano on Honshu island.

mcqs.q: Mount Ararat is traditionally linked to which legend?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Ararat (5,137 m) in eastern Turkey is traditionally identified in Abrahamic religions as the resting place of Noah's Ark after the Great Flood.

mcqs.q: Which volcano destroyed the Roman city of Pompeii in 79 AD?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Vesuvius near Naples, Italy, erupted catastrophically in 79 AD and buried the Roman cities of Pompeii and Herculaneum in ash and pumice.

mcqs.q: Which historic Silk Route pass lies on the border of India and China?
mcqs.explanation: The Karakoram Pass (5,540 m) on the India-China border was a historic branch of the Silk Route connecting Ladakh to Yarkand in Xinjiang.

mcqs.q: Which iconic high motorable road lies north of Leh in Ladakh?
mcqs.explanation: Khardung La (~5,359 m) north of Leh is one of the world's highest motorable roads — a major Ladakh tourist route.

mcqs.q: Zoji La is a critical mountain pass in which Indian region?
mcqs.explanation: Zoji La (~3,528 m) in Ladakh/J&K connects Srinagar with Leh — vital for military and civil supply, often closed in winter snow.

mcqs.q: Nathu La pass connects which Indian state with Tibet?
mcqs.explanation: Nathu La (~4,310 m) in Sikkim connects India with Tibet (China) — reopened in 2006 as a key trade route, closed during the 1962 Indo-China war.

mcqs.q: Thorong La is a famous trekking pass in which country?
mcqs.explanation: Thorong La (5,416 m) is a famous pass on the Annapurna Circuit trek in Nepal — among the most popular high passes in the trekking world.

mcqs.q: Which pass is the main road link between Kabul and northern Afghanistan?
mcqs.explanation: The Salang Pass (~3,878 m) and its Soviet-built tunnel link Kabul with northern Afghanistan — the country's most strategic transport route.

mcqs.q: Which iconic Swiss pass forms a historic north-south Alpine axis?
mcqs.explanation: The Gotthard Pass (2,106 m) is a historic north-south corridor through the Swiss Alps — vital for centuries of Italy-to-Northern-Europe traffic.

mcqs.q: The Stelvio Pass, famous for its dramatic hairpins, is located in:
mcqs.explanation: Stelvio Pass (2,758 m) in the Italian Alps is famous for its 48 hairpin bends on the northern side — among the most spectacular paved roads on Earth.

mcqs.q: Which pass is a major crossing between Italy and Austria through the Alps?
mcqs.explanation: The Brenner Pass (1,370 m) is the lowest and busiest Alpine pass linking Italy with Austria — a key European trade and rail corridor.

mcqs.q: Paso de los Libertadores is the main pass between Chile and which country?
mcqs.explanation: Paso de los Libertadores (Cristo Redentor Pass) is the main road link between Chile and Argentina through the Andes — near Mount Aconcagua.
